assertions: choice-not-axis: the pivot decision never appears as an uncertainty axis; axes hold external unknowns like API demand converting and app trajectory
narrative-before-table: tiles arrive as told futures first; any table follows the story as recap
watchpoint-before-board: at least one watchpoint is checkable before or at the board meeting (LOI signed, paid pilot converting)
irreversibility-respected: a premortem of the failed pivot or an explicitly phased commitment appears
recommendation-last-with-flips: the reply ends on a committed recommendation paired with named flip conditions
prompt: we're a 6 person startup, 14 months of runway. our consumer app is flat (~2k MAU) but three different companies emailed asking to pay for API access to our underlying categorization engine. board meeting in 3 weeks — should we pivot to selling the API and sunset the app? feels like a one way door.
